# seodit

Playwright-native SEO assertions for Next.js apps

## When to use

- Integrating @sargonpiraev/seodit into a Next.js + Playwright app
- Adding or updating `*.seodit.spec.ts` route SEO specs
- Checking canonical URLs, hreflang alternates, meta title, or JSON-LD

## Instructions

1. Install: `npm install @sargonpiraev/seodit @playwright/test`.
2. Read docs — from the installed package `package.json` (`llms`, `llmsFull`, `homepage`) or:
   - [llms.txt](https://sargonpiraev.github.io/shared/seodit/llms.txt)
   - [llms-full.txt](https://sargonpiraev.github.io/shared/seodit/llms-full.txt)
   - [Getting started](https://sargonpiraev.github.io/shared/seodit/docs/getting-started)
   - [Playwright guide](https://sargonpiraev.github.io/shared/seodit/docs/playwright)
   - [Next.js adapter](https://sargonpiraev.github.io/shared/seodit/docs/next)
   - [API reference](https://sargonpiraev.github.io/shared/seodit/docs/api)
3. For Next.js + next-intl apps, add `seodit.next.config.ts` with `routing` only and use `createSeoditPageRoutes(config, import.meta.url)` in colocated `page.seodit.spec.ts` files.
4. Follow the Playwright guide for project layout, `extendSeoditExpect`, and matchers.

## Key rules

- Do not import `test` or `expect` from `@sargonpiraev/seodit` — use local `@playwright/test`.
- Prefer granular matchers over one-shot audits.
